title: Trump Hits the Stalemate Phase of His Interventions in Gaza, Ukraine and Now Iran
summary: In Ukraine, Gaza and now Iran, President Trump’s early declarations of easy wins have given way to harsh reality.

title: U.S. Military Is Quietly Guiding Ships Through the Strait of Hormuz
summary: U.S. Central Command has helped around 70 commercial ships pass through the strait in the last three weeks, an official said.

title: Israel Captures Crusader Castle That Symbolized Its Long Lebanon Occupation
summary: The seizure of Beaufort in southern Lebanon called up bitter memories in both countries amid a widening Israeli conflict with Hezbollah that seems far from over.

title: Trump Administration Sees Striking Exodus of Legal Talent
summary: The departure of more than 10,000 federal lawyers has left some agencies without sufficient staff and has boosted the ranks of state attorneys general offices and advocacy groups.

title: Trump Is Dominating GOP Primary Elections. Does That Mean Victory in the Midterms?
summary: Come November, the Republican Party will need the support of voters outside of President Trump’s base, many of whom are deeply dissatisfied with the economy and the Iran war.

title: Release of Tina Peters, Election Denier and Former County Clerk, Divides Colorado City
summary: The former clerk of Mesa County is scheduled to be set free on Monday, after her nine-year prison sentence was commuted by Gov. Jared Polis.

title: Cory Booker Has ‘Concerns’ About Platner Revelations in Maine Senate Race
summary: Mr. Booker and other Senate Democrats faced questions Sunday about reports that Mr. Platner’s wife previously told his campaign he had exchanged sexual messages with women outside his marriage.

title: He Was Satirized on British TV. Now He’s Trump’s Pick to Lead California.
summary: Steve Hilton grabbed headlines when he worked in conservative politics in Britain. His American political renaissance in the California governor’s race has bemused former British colleagues and rivals.

title: In U.F.O. Files, Some Christians See Vexing Questions — and Demons
summary: The prospect of intelligent life elsewhere in the universe raises unsettling theological implications.

title: Tilly Norwood, A.I. Actress, Wants to Know Why Everyone’s Mad at Her
summary: The A.I. actress on her craft, the future of film and how she definitely does not intend to murder us.

title: India’s Hindu Right Has a New Hero: A 17th-Century Warrior King
summary: Statues of Shivaji are rising everywhere. The founder of the Maratha empire who fought against the Mughal dynasty is now a symbol for nationalists remaking the country.

title: The Sculptor Riding India’s Shivaji Craze
summary: Demand has soared for his figurines and statues of the warrior king. It started with a video he posted online.

title: Havoc in Hawaii’s Coffee Fields After an Earthquake Destroyed Water Collection Tanks
summary: An earthquake destroyed many water catchment systems on Hawaii’s Big Island, capping a spring filled with devastating floods.

title: In Taiwan, ‘Mainland Spouses’ From China Become a Focus of Infiltration Fears
summary: At the heart of a criminal case involving one such wife is a question: How can Taiwan protect its democracy without unfairly targeting Chinese migrants?

title: Joby Demonstrated its Air Taxi in Manhattan, but You Can’t Fly in It Yet
summary: Aviation start-ups and the Trump administration want to replace helicopters with electric aircraft, but the new vehicles still have to pass arduous tests before the public can use them.

title: Colombia Presidential Election Heads to a Runoff
summary: The candidate forced a runoff on Sunday in what could herald another gain for the right-wing wave sweeping Latin America.

title: How Your Friend Group Influences How You Spend and Save
summary: Our spending habits are often influenced by our social circles. You can help shape what it looks like.

title: The Museum of Wretched Ideas Has Reopened
summary: We seem to have forgotten more or less everything.

title: Time Gave Me Back What Prison Took Away
summary: A formerly incarcerated writer reflects on how the prison system didn’t foster change, but befriending time did.

title: High-Speed Sailboats Race Past the New York Skyline
summary: SailGP, a two-day competition some say is like Formula 1 on the water, has replaced part of the normal traffic in New York Harbor with high-tech catamarans.

title: 2025 Wildfires Were the Costliest Ever, Researchers Say
summary: Severe, hard-to-control blazes in densely populated areas like Los Angeles drove the year’s record losses.

title: Notre Dame Students Abused by Priest in ‘Weighing Scheme,’ Report Finds
summary: An investigation commissioned by Notre Dame found that the Catholic university could have done more to respond to allegations of sexual misconduct.

title: The World Capital of French Fries Has a Problem: Too Many Potatoes
summary: Belgium’s potato harvest set a record, just as tariffs hit U.S. demand for frozen fries and as competition from suppliers in Asia intensified.
